senses.definition: (of someone in authority) to find out that someone did something wrong and decide to do something about it

examples.x: Eventually, the legal system caught up with the fraudulent activities of the businessman.
The inspector assured the workers that safety standards would catch up with those who violated them.
The law eventually caught up with the fugitive, leading to his arrest.
The police caught up with the suspect after days of investigation.
The boss caught up with the team members who were responsible for the project delay.

senses.definition: to eject stomach contents through the mouth

examples.x: After eating spoiled food, he felt nauseous and eventually had to chuck up.
The seasick passenger had to chuck up over the side of the boat during the rough journey.
After a night of heavy drinking, he felt the need to chuck up in the bathroom.
The flu made her feel so unwell that she had to chuck up multiple times throughout the day.
The spicy food didn't agree with her stomach, and she had to chuck up shortly after the meal.

senses.definition: to find something by excavating or digging in the ground

examples.x: The archaeologists hoped to dig up evidence of an ancient civilization in the dig site.
While gardening, she was surprised to dig up an old coin buried in the soil.
The treasure hunters believed they could dig up long-lost pirate treasures on the island.
The construction crew had to pause their work when they accidentally dug up a buried pipeline.
The amateur archaeologist was thrilled to dig up a fossilized bone in his backyard.

senses.definition: to make oneself look beautiful or stylish, especially for a special event

examples.x: She decided to doll up for the party, wearing an elegant gown and accessorizing with jewelry.
The bride and her bridesmaids took the morning to doll up in preparation for the wedding ceremony.
Wanting to make a memorable impression, he chose to doll up in a sharp suit and polished shoes.

senses.definition: to wear formal clothes for a special occasion or event

examples.x: They decided to dress up for the elegant gala, donning evening gowns and tuxedos.
The children were excited to dress up in costumes for the Halloween party.
It's customary to dress up in traditional attire for cultural celebrations.
The couple chose to dress up for their anniversary dinner at a fancy restaurant.
Attending the wedding, guests were expected to dress up in semi-formal attire.

senses.definition: to improve one's appearance, energy, or smell, usually by washing, grooming, or getting refreshed

examples.x: A quick shower is an excellent way to freshen up in the morning.
Before the party, they freshened up their appearances by getting dressed and styling their hair.

senses.definition: to try to find information in a dictionary, computer, etc.

examples.x: Wait a minute, I'm looking up the address.
You can look up any unfamiliar term on the internet.
You should look up the word to improve your vocabulary.

senses.definition: to improve one's appearance by wearing more stylish or formal clothing

examples.x: Before the wedding ceremony, the groomsmen had to smarten up their suits for a polished look.
Realizing it was a formal occasion, she decided to smarten up her hairstyle and makeup.
Feeling underdressed, he rushed to smarten up his appearance before the formal event.
He decided to smarten up for the job interview by wearing a crisp suit and tie.

senses.definition: to bring up stomach contents through the mouth

examples.x: The baby spit up after feeding, and the parents quickly cleaned the mess.
Feeling unwell, he suddenly spit up his lunch in the restroom.
The motion sickness caused her to spit up during the car ride.
After consuming spoiled food, he couldn't help but spit up, feeling nauseous.

senses.definition: to expel the contents of the stomach through the mouth

examples.x: The bumpy car ride made the motion-sick passenger throw up.
The roller coaster was so intense that many people on board threw up.
She had a stomach bug and spent the night throwing up.
The seasickness caused several passengers on the ship to throw up.
The bad odor in the room made her feel sick, and she had to throw up.
